todayPlan.note: 以下任务未能完全排入DDL前：数学卷

details.rationale: Task must be split due to continuous-time cap (45 min) and daily load cap (90 min). With only two available slots (Mon 07:00–08:00 and Tue 07:00–08:00), each 60 min, and mandatory 10-min gaps between segments, feasible segmentation is: Day 1 (Mon): 45 min (07:00–07:45); gap until 07:55; then 15 min (07:55–08:10) — but slot ends at 08:00 → violates slot boundary. Therefore, only one 45-min segment fits per day without overflow. To complete 120 min, need ≥3 segments → requires ≥3 days, but deadline is 2026-06-29 and window ends then → impossible under current constraints. Risk is unavoidable conflict between time-bound constraints and task duration.
risks: Available slots total only 60 minutes across planning window (Mon–Fri 07:00–08:00 = 5 × 60 min = 300 min, but window is only 2026-06-28 to 2026-06-29 → only Mon Jun 28 and Tue Jun 29 are in window; both have one 60-min slot → 120 total minutes available), yet task requires 120 minutes and must be completed by deadline 2026-06-29 — tight fit. However, rule_test_paper_continuous mandates ≤45 min continuous, and rule_multi_set_rest mandates ≥10 min gap between segments; thus 120-min task must be split into ≥3 segments (e.g., 45+45+30), requiring ≥2 gaps → minimum schedule footprint = 45+10+45+10+30 = 140 minutes. But only 120 minutes available across two days → insufficient slot capacity to satisfy all constraints.

Cognitive load constraint 'rule_cognitive_load_daily_cap' limits high-load tasks to ≤90 min/day; splitting 120 min across two days (e.g., 60+60) violates this if both segments are classified as 'high' load — and test_paper tasks are explicitly tagged high cognitive load per planning_constraints.
空闲时段不足，部分任务无法在DDL前完成。
总需求 120 分钟，高于可用时段 60 分钟。
已安排 60 / 120 分钟，建议压缩任务或增加空闲时段。

taskEstimates.title: 数学卷
taskEstimates.reason: Baseline parametric estimate is 120 minutes (p50), supported by high-confidence calibration case case_math_test_paper_exam_sprint_medium_003 (118 min) and template tpl_math_test_paper_standard (120 min). Adjustment beyond ±15% is not justified despite warning about case_math_test_paper_middle_high_medium_052 (88 min), as that case reflects lower-grade band and lower cognitive load; user profile p_type_high_school_student aligns with senior_high grade_band in evidence_package, so 120 remains appropriate.
